$comment: Per-secondary-entry-point size budget for the SHIPPED Caelum library (Book 18 §3.2 — the OTHER size gate). Measured by scripts/check-lib-size.mjs as the gzipped byte size of each dist/caelum/fesm2022/*.mjs. angular.json `budgets` measure only the Forge APPLICATION build (esbuild), NOT ng-packagr output — hence this bespoke gate. It defends the 'pay only for what you import' contract: importing cae-select must not drag in cae-tree. A NEW entry point with no budget row here FAILS the gate — set its budget deliberately (the forward ratchet). Since #28 the monolith is split: `caelum` is now a THIN re-export barrel (it re-exports the per-component entry points by name, so it carries no component code — ~0.7kb of re-export shims) and every `caelum-<name>` row below is one tree-shakable component — EXCEPT `caelum-form-field`, a runtime abstract-directive base (`CaeFormFieldControlBase`, #46/#135, generic over its value type) shared by the five form-field controls (cae-input/textarea/select/autocomplete/multi-select): like `caelum-shared` it is not a component, but unlike it, it carries runtime code. Budgets carry ~15-30% headroom over the measured size so a refactor doesn't trip the gate, but real growth (e.g. #29 adds error-forwarding to input) BUMPS the affected row deliberately; a new component/adapter adds its own row. Extracting shared code (#46 moved the CVA + error-forwarding out of input/select/textarea into caelum-form-field) SHRINKS the source rows — retighten them to the new measurement. Two rows are deliberately TIGHT because their size is itself an invariant: `caelum` (the barrel) must stay a thin re-export shim — a budget near its measured ~0.7kb catches a bad merge that inlines a component into it; `caelum-shared` must stay type-only (near-zero runtime) — a tight budget trips the moment a real runtime symbol is added to it. Book 18 §4b illustrative targets: caelum-select ~12kb, caelum-data-grid ~40kb (the M2 adapters land far heavier than these thin Direct wrappers) — but caelum-grid measures ~13kb gzip, NOT ~40kb, because the TanStack engine is left EXTERNAL (an OPTIONAL peerDependency `@tanstack/table-core`, imported by grid.adapter.ts, not inlined into the fesm), so the ~14kb engine cost is paid once by the consumer's app build ONLY when they opt into provideTanStackGrid. Client-default consumers ship zero engine bytes VIA STANDARD TREE-SHAKING (both `caelum` and `@tanstack/table-core` declare sideEffects:false, so an unreferenced re-export is dropped) — this measured budget guards the fesm, and Forge proves the DEFERRAL path (table-core verified absent from the eager chunk); a dedicated consumer-tree-shake bundling guard is filed as a followup. The #171 bump 11264->16384 covers the TanStack adapter's own source + JSDoc, not the engine. The #176 bump 16384->20480 covers the dependency-free ServerGridAdapter (the lazy/remote engine, `server-grid-adapter.ts`) — its own source + JSDoc, no engine (it imports no grid library, like the client default). The #192 bump 20480->24576 covers the loading-blocks-interaction + [caeGridBusy] slot work on `data-grid.ts` (aria-disabled sort/pager, the busy overlay, and the expanded [loading] JSDoc that ships in the fesm) — measured ~19kb gzip, restoring ~20% headroom. The #202 bump caelum-chip-set 5120->6656 covers the optional [emptyFocusTarget] empty-set focus hook on `chip-set.ts` (the input + onRemoved handler + the review-expanded JSDoc that ships in the fesm — gate/sync-drop/live-region caveats) — measured ~5.4kb gzip, restoring ~19% headroom. The #143 bump caelum-table 6400->10240 covers the custom cell-template seam (`cell-def.ts`: the CaeCellDef directive + CaeCellContext, the NgTemplateOutlet per-cell rendering with template-or-text fallback, the typo/duplicate dev-warn effect, and the p-table-parity + accessible-cell-content JSDoc that ships in the fesm) — measured ~7.7kb gzip, restoring ~25% headroom. The #144 bump caelum-table 10240->11264 covers row selection (selectionMode='none'|'multiple' + a vendor-neutral [(selection)] model<T[]> + the prepended select-all/row mat-checkbox column + selectedSet/allSelected/someSelected/toggleRow/toggleAll + rowSelectionLabel/selectAllLabel a11y accessors + the reserved-key validateConfig guard + JSDoc; MatCheckbox stays external, not inlined) — measured ~9.3kb gzip, restoring ~18% headroom. The #213 bump caelum-table 11264->12288 covers the absolute row-index context field (a CaeCellContext.absoluteIndex + the pageOffset signal tracked off the paginator's page event + JSDoc) — measured ~9.9kb gzip, restoring ~17% headroom. The #144 single-select bump caelum-table 12288->15360 covers single-select mode (selectionMode='single' + a radio column via MatRadioModule [external, not inlined — only the template branch/selectOne/radioName/JSDoc add bytes], the shared-name native radio-group a11y, a self-managed roving [tabIndex] so the group is one tab stop not N, a visually-hidden selection-column header for AT, and the review-expanded a11y JSDoc) — measured ~12.2kb gzip, restoring ~18% headroom. The #224 bump caelum-table 15360->16384 covers opt-in click/Space-to-deselect for single-select (the `allowDeselect` input + the `onRadioActivate` re-activation handler + the two `(click)`/`(keydown.space)` radio bindings + the deviation JSDoc that ships in the fesm) — measured ~13.0kb gzip, restoring ~18% headroom. The #194 bump caelum-grid 24576->28672 covers the born-empty status live region (a `rendered` signal + afterNextRender, and the clip-based visually-hidden empty state that keeps role=status registered in the a11y tree instead of display:none) + JSDoc — measured ~22.3kb gzip, restoring ~22% headroom. The #144 expandable-rows bump caelum-table 16384->20480 covers expandable detail rows (a vendor-neutral [(expanded)] model<T[]> + the prepended accessible expand-toggle column via MatButtonModule [mat-icon-button, bundled into this fesm] with the aria-expanded/aria-controls disclosure pattern + the multiTemplateDataRows full-width detail row with a projected `caeRowDetailDef` template (`row-detail-def.ts`: the CaeRowDetailDef directive + CaeRowDetailContext) + isExpanded/toggleExpand/detailId WeakMap + the expand/detail reserved-key guard + the a11y JSDoc that ships in the fesm) — measured ~16.2kb gzip, restoring ~19% headroom. The #144 sticky bump caelum-table 20480->24576 covers sticky header + sticky columns (the `stickyHeader` input + the `sticky`/`stickyEnd` pass-throughs on each column def + the `leadColumnSticky` computed that auto-freezes the built-in expand/select columns behind a frozen leading data column [#252, a WCAG 2.4.11 focus-occlusion fix] + the reactive `warnOnStickyConfig` dev-guards for both-edges and non-contiguous sticky runs, whose warning STRINGS ship in the fesm because the isDevMode() gate is a runtime check + the sticky/scroll-context a11y JSDoc) — no CSS of our own (Material's .mat-mdc-table-sticky supplies position:sticky and the inherited opaque background) — measured ~20.0kb gzip, restoring ~17% headroom. The #273 NEW row caelum-carousel 11264 is the first ★ media-family entry point (Book 11 §3.4) — a content-agnostic rotating carousel: the signal sliding-window index model + circular/autoplay(pause-on-hover/focus, WCAG 2.2.2 play/pause, prefers-reduced-motion no-autostart) + prev/next + roving-tabindex indicator group + full APG carousel ARIA + the caeCarouselItem projected-slide directive + its inline CSS-drawn glyphs (no icon font) + JSDoc; NgTemplateOutlet is the only Angular import, no Material — measured ~9.5kb gzip, ~16% headroom (2-lens review dropped the inert responsiveOptions input, switched indicator focus to a viewChildren query, and added the aria-disabled-nav + numScroll-clamp + SSR-timer-guard fixes). The #279 NEW row caelum-tree-select 8704 is the ★ value-picker family's tree control (Book 09 §3.5, `p-treeSelect` parity) — a hierarchical single/multi node-select whose panel is the library's FIRST cdkConnectedOverlay: a mat-tree (rendering + roving-tabindex + expand/collapse from the CDK tree, like cae-tree) + a selection Set + cdkTrapFocus (focus-in on open, restore-to-trigger on dismiss); value = node key(s) via NG_VALUE_ACCESSOR (the #47 consumer-owned-error pattern for selection controls, NOT the mat-form-field base — a custom overlay has no inner Material control to bridge), reusing the CaeTreeNode model type-only (erased, no bundle coupling); A11yModule + OverlayModule + MatTreeModule stay external, not inlined — measured ~8.0kb gzip after the 2-lens review (8704->9728, ~16% headroom) — the review shipped the aria-controls-to-the-role=tree fix, single-mode aria-selected-only-on-selected, an empty-tree focus-strand guard, setDisabledState-without-touched, a selectionMode set-once dev-warn, and a single-select no-op re-emit guard (deferring focus-selected-on-open #281, multi-select live region #281, showClear/filter/dup-key-warn #282, lazy/virtual/mat-form-field #283). The caelum barrel row 960->1024 absorbs the tree-select re-export shim (+3 B — legitimate thin-shim growth, still tight enough to catch a bad component inline, which would add KBs not bytes). The #274 NEW row caelum-galleria 8704 (measured ~7.1kb gzip, ~16% headroom) is the ★ media family's image gallery (Book 11 §3.4, p-galleria parity) — a main image view + prev/next navigators + a thumbnail tablist (WAI-ARIA tabs, roving tabindex, selection-follows-focus) + a polite position live region + a fullscreen lightbox opened through caelum/dialog (D-15): the internal CaeGalleriaLightbox component (large image + prev/next + counter + Escape/backdrop close, all on Material's centered modal) ships in this fesm, but @angular/material/dialog and caelum/dialog stay EXTERNAL (imported, not inlined). v1 renders the full thumbnail strip (fully keyboard-verifiable); cdk-virtual-scroll virtualization for very large galleries is deferred to a follow-up gated on the #240 browser runner. The barrel row stays 1024 (the +3 B galleria re-export shim fits its existing headroom).
